title: Peppery Dutch Cheese Flatbread
description: A bold, pepperladen flatbread packed with Dutch Gouda and a hint of smoked paprika, perfect for a lowcarb adventurous meal.
instructions: In a large bowl whisk together the flour, salt, and sugar.
In a separate container combine the warm water, active dry yeast, and a pinch of sugar; let sit for 5 minutes until foamy.
Pour the liquid mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Add the olive oil and continue mixing until the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl, about 3–4 minutes.
Transfer dough to a floured surface, knead for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and allow it to rise in a warm place for 30 minutes until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 220 °C (428 °F). Punch down the dough and roll it into a flat circle about 25 cm (10 in) in diameter and 5 mm thick.
Transfer the dough to a parchment‑lined baking sheet. Brush the top lightly with olive oil, then sprinkle evenly with grated Gouda, crushed peppercorns, smoked paprika, and rosemary.
Bake for 10–12 minutes until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbly.
Remove from the oven, let cool for 2 minutes, slice, and serve hot.
